data.title: JPMorgan CEO Warns of Potential Credit Market Downturn
data.description: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon has issued a warning that the credit market downturn could be more severe than anticipated, despite strong quarterly results from his bank and Wall Street competitors. According to Jin10, Dimon spoke at a meeting hosted by Norway's central bank investment management company on Tuesday, highlighting concerns in the private credit sector, which includes over 1,000 companies. He suggested that while some companies may perform exceptionally well, not all will withstand a cyclical reversal. Dimon emphasized that underwriting standards have not faced a credit recession in a long time, and when the next one occurs, it could be worse than expected. He added that this situation might also affect some banks adversely.

data.title: USDC Treasury Mints 250 Million USDC
data.description: According to NS3.AI, Whale Alert has reported the minting of 250 million USDC at the USDC Treasury. This development is significant in the context of the cryptocurrency market, as such large-scale minting activities can influence market liquidity and investor sentiment. The minting of USDC, a stablecoin pegged to the U.S. dollar, often reflects changes in demand for stable digital assets. Market participants will be closely monitoring the impact of this minting on the broader cryptocurrency ecosystem.

data.title: Michael Terpin Predicts Bitcoin Low Near $57,000 in October
data.description: Michael Terpin has expressed his view that Bitcoin has not yet reached its lowest point, forecasting a potential dip to around $57,000 sometime in October. According to NS3.AI, Terpin also mentioned that achieving a new all-time high for Bitcoin is unlikely this year.

data.title: U.S. President Trump and King Charles III to Discuss UK-US Relations
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump and King Charles III are scheduled to meet on Tuesday to discuss the state of the "special relationship" between the United States and the United Kingdom. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the talks come at a time when the bilateral ties are perceived to be at a low point. The meeting aims to address and potentially strengthen the longstanding alliance between the two nations.

data.title: Circle Launches USDC and CCTP on Pharos
data.description: Circle has announced the launch of its USDC and Circle Cross-Chain Transfer Protocol (CCTP) on the Pharos platform. According to Foresight News, this development aims to enhance the interoperability and functionality of digital currencies across different blockchain networks. The integration is expected to facilitate seamless transactions and improve the efficiency of digital asset transfers.

data.title: TON Tech Introduces AI Agents for Telegram to Enhance Blockchain Transactions
data.description: TON Tech has unveiled AI agents designed for Telegram, a platform with approximately 1 billion users, to facilitate onchain transfers, swaps, and staking. According to NS3.AI, these agents allow users to fund a dedicated wallet, enabling automated trading within predefined budgets and basic portfolio management. This launch occurs amid a decline in TON blockchain activity, which has dropped below 100,000 active users after surpassing 1 million in late 2024.

data.title: Polymarket Seeks CFTC Approval for U.S. Platform Launch
data.description: Polymarket is seeking approval from the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) to introduce its main trading platform in the United States. According to Foresight News, this move aims to expand Polymarket's presence in the U.S. market. The company is working to comply with regulatory requirements to facilitate its entry into the American financial landscape. This development follows ongoing efforts by Polymarket to align with U.S. regulations and enhance its service offerings.

data.title: Iranian Military on High Alert Amid Ongoing Tensions
data.description: Iran's Tasnim News Agency reported on the 28th that the Iranian army spokesperson, Mohammad Akraminia, stated that the military considers the current situation to be in a state of war. According to Odaily, ground forces have been deployed nationwide, ready to respond to threats. Akraminia noted that Iran has completed a comprehensive update of its target list and operational equipment, ensuring all forces are fully prepared to counter potential military attacks.

data.title: Polymarket Plans Return to U.S. Amid Industry Growth
data.description: Polymarket is making efforts to reintroduce its primary exchange to the United States. According to Bloomberg, individuals familiar with the situation suggest that this move could significantly enhance the growth of the industry by allowing American users to access the platform. The initiative aims to capitalize on the expanding market and increase participation from U.S. users.

data.title: Fidelity deposited 19,934 ETH into Coinbase, worth $45.29 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain, Fidelity deposited 19,934 ETH, worth $45.29 million, into Coinbase 30 minutes ago.

data.title: Iranian Army Spokesperson: The war is not over yet, and the army is on high alert.
data.description: Iran's Tasnim News Agency reported on the 28th, citing Iranian Army spokesman Mohammad Akraminia, that the military considers the current situation to be still at war, and ground troops have been deployed throughout the country, ready to respond to threats. He stated that Iran has completed a comprehensive update of its target list and combat equipment, and all troops are fully prepared to deal with possible enemy military attacks. (Xinhua)

data.title: A Matrixport-linked whale, which profited over $59 million, went long on 30,000 ETH.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale associated with Matrixport, which profited over $59 million, opened a long position of 30,000 ETH with 15x leverage in the past hour, worth $68 million. Currently, this whale holds a total of 58,000 ETH long positions through three different addresses (0xa5B0...1D41, 0xfd42...3d97, and 0x6c85...84f6), with leverage ratios ranging from 15 to 20x, and a total holding value of $131.82 million.

data.title: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon Expresses Confidence in U.S. Economy
data.description: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon has expressed confidence in the stability of the U.S. economy. According to NS3.AI, Dimon stated that he is not concerned about the current economic conditions in the United States. His remarks come amid ongoing discussions about economic growth and potential challenges facing the financial sector. Dimon's perspective offers reassurance to investors and stakeholders who are closely monitoring economic indicators and market trends.

data.title: Iran to Submit Revised Peace Proposal Soon
data.description: Iran is expected to soon submit a revised peace proposal, according to Odaily. This information comes from unnamed sources familiar with the matter.

data.title: Guggenheim's John DiFucci: Microsoft's Fiscal Q3 May Present Mixed Results
data.description: John DiFucci of Guggenheim has expressed that Microsoft's fiscal third quarter could yield mixed results ahead of the company's earnings report on April 29. According to NS3.AI, DiFucci has maintained a price target of $586 for Microsoft (MSFT). He noted that Wall Street's expectations for Azure's constant-currency growth, projected at 37%–38%, might be overly optimistic, despite the generally positive sentiment among analysts.

data.title: Sources: Iran is expected to submit a revised peace proposal soon.
data.description: Sources say Iran is expected to submit a revised peace proposal soon. (Jinshi)

data.title: Elon Musk arrived at a California court to make his opening statement in his lawsuit against OpenAI.
data.description: Elon Musk arrived at the U.S. District Court in Oakland, California, to make his opening statement in his lawsuit against OpenAI.

data.title: Fed's Final Powell Meeting Faces Stagflation Dilemma as Rate Cut Path Hangs in the Balance
data.description:
Key Takeaways
The Fed's two-day policy meeting concludes Thursday in what will be Jerome Powell's last as chair, with rates widely expected to hold at 3.50%–3.75%The critical question is not whether rates hold but whether policymakers signal rate cuts are derailed or merely delayedFed Governor Waller, who backed three rate cuts last year over labor market concerns, has shifted to wariness about inflation risks this monthThe US is experiencing its fourth supply shock in five years -- pandemic reopening, Russia-Ukraine, the tariff war, and now the Middle East conflictFed officials do not expect inflation to return to the 2% target for at least another year, with the Strait of Hormuz still effectively blocked and jet fuel prices surging
The Federal Reserve concludes its two-day policy meeting Thursday in what will be Jerome Powell's final session as chair, with markets locked in on not the rate decision itself -- a hold at 3.50%–3.75% is essentially certain -- but the language policymakers choose to describe the path ahead for rate cuts amid a resurgent stagflation threat.
Nick Timiraos, the Wall Street Journal reporter widely followed as a conduit for Fed thinking, wrote on April 28 that the central debate inside the Fed is over policy wording and whether officials will characterize the current pause as a temporary delay or a more significant derailment of the rate cut trajectory signaled earlier this year.
The Fourth Supply Shock in Five Years
The backdrop for Thursday's decision is unusually complicated. The US economy is now navigating its fourth distinct supply shock in five years: the post-pandemic economic reopening, the Russia-Ukraine conflict, the tariff war, and now the Middle East energy disruption triggered by the Iran conflict. Each has forced the Fed to recalibrate its inflation and growth outlook in real time, and the current shock arrives at a particularly sensitive moment -- with inflation still above target, growth slowing, and energy prices surging.
WTI crude has broken above $103 per barrel and Brent has topped $105, with jet fuel prices soaring as the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively blocked to normal commercial traffic despite a nominal ceasefire. The energy shock is feeding directly into inflation expectations, complicating the Fed's ability to pivot toward easing even as growth risks mount -- a classic stagflationary dynamic.
Waller's Pivot Signals Internal Shift
One of the clearest indicators of the Fed's internal recalibration comes from Governor Christopher Waller, who as recently as last year supported three rate cuts driven by concerns about labor market softening. This month, Waller has turned notably cautious on inflation risks -- a significant shift from one of the more dovish voices on the board that suggests the stagflation concern is gaining traction inside the institution.
Fed officials broadly do not expect inflation to return to the 2% target for at least another year, a timeline that effectively rules out near-term rate cuts unless growth deteriorates sharply enough to override the inflation constraint.
What Markets Are Watching
With the rate decision itself a non-event, the entire market focus falls on the post-meeting statement and Powell's final press conference as chair. The key signal traders are watching: will the Fed describe the current pause as a temporary delay in an ongoing easing cycle, or will it shift language in a way that suggests the rate cut path has been meaningfully pushed back?
For crypto markets, the distinction is significant. Bitcoin has been partially supported by expectations of eventual Fed easing, and any language that signals a more prolonged hold could remove a key pillar of the current bull narrative. The meeting also carries symbolic weight as Powell's final act as chair before Kevin Warsh's confirmation hearing -- adding a layer of institutional transition uncertainty to an already complex policy backdrop.

data.title: Sharplink Reports Staking Rewards of 459 ETH This Week
data.description: Sharplink announced that it received 459 ETH in staking rewards this week. According to ChainCatcher, the company's total staking earnings have reached 18,309 ETH to date.
